functions.name: FSEvents.FlushASync
functions.description: Flushes events asynchronously. Asks the FS Events service to flush out any events that have occurred but have not yet been delivered, due to the latency parameter that was supplied when the stream was created.  This flushing occurs asynchronously -- do not expect the events to have already been delivered by the time this call returns. FSEvents.FlushAsync can only be called after the stream has been started, via FSEvents.Start .

parameters.name: FSEventsRef
parameters.description: The reference number for this FSEvents object.

functions.name: FSEvents.FlushSync
functions.description: Flushes events synchronously. Asks the FS Events service to flush out any events that have occurred but have not yet been delivered, due to the latency parameter that was supplied when the stream was created.  This flushing occurs synchronously -- by the time this call returns, your callback will have been invoked for every event that had already occurred at the time you made this call. FSEvents.FlushSync can only be called after the stream has been started, via FSEvents.Start .

parameters.name: FSEventsRef
parameters.description: The reference number for this FSEvents object.

examples.label: Flush events:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FSEvents.FlushSync"; $FSEvents )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FSEvents.LastEventIdForDeviceBeforeTime
functions.description: Queries event ID for a given time on a device. Gets the last event ID for the given device that was returned before the given time.  This is conservative in the sense that if you then use the returned event ID as the sinceWhen parameter of FSEvents.CreateRelativeToDevice that you will not miss any events that happened since that time.  On the other hand, you might receive some (harmless) extra events. Beware: there are things that can cause this to fail to be accurate. For example, someone might change the system's clock (either backwards or forwards).  Or an external drive might be used on different systems without perfectly synchronized clocks.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the disk.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp to use.

functions.name: FSEvents.NextEvent
functions.description: Queries next event in queue. Returns a list of values. First entry is the path, second entry the ID number for this event. Third is the flags value as number and forth as text values. This allows you to check for some values like "ItemCreated". Flags that are returned for file system events. It is important to note that event flags are simply hints about the sort of operations that occurred at that path. Furthermore, the FSEvent stream should NOT be treated as a form of historical log that could somehow be replayed to arrive at the current state of the file system. The FSEvent stream simply indicates what paths changed; and clients need to reconcile what is really in the file system with their internal data model - and recognize that what is actually in the file system can change immediately after you check it. MustScanSubDirs = 1 Your application must rescan not just the directory given in the event, but all its children, recursively. This can happen if there was a problem whereby events were coalesced hierarchically. For example, an event in /Users/jsmith/Music and an event in /Users/jsmith/Pictures might be coalesced into an event with this flag set and path=/Users/jsmith. If this flag is set you may be able to get an idea of whether the bottleneck happened in the kernel (less likely) or in your client (more likely) by checking for the presence of the informational flags UserDropped or KernelDropped. UserDropped = 2 KernelDropped = 4 The UserDropped or KernelDropped flags may be set in addition to the MustScanSubDirs flag to indicate that a problem occurred in buffering the events (the particular flag set indicates where the problem occurred) and that the client must do a full scan of any directories (and their subdirectories, recursively) being monitored by this stream. If you asked to monitor multiple paths with this stream then you will be notified about all of them. Your code need only check for the MustScanSubDirs flag; these flags (if present) only provide information to help you diagnose the problem. EventIdsWrapped = 8 If EventIdsWrapped is set, it means the 64-bit event ID counter wrapped around. As a result, previously-issued event ID's are no longer valid arguments for the sinceWhen parameter of the FSEvent.Create function. HistoryDone = 16 Denotes a sentinel event sent to mark the end of the "historical" events sent as a result of specifying a sinceWhen value in the FSEvents.Create ...() call that created this event stream. (It will not be sent if kFSEventStreamEventIdSinceNow was passed for sinceWhen.) After invoking the client's callback with all the "historical" events that occurred before now, the client's callback will be invoked with an event where the HistoryDone flag is set. The client should ignore the path supplied in this callback. RootChanged = 32 Denotes a special event sent when there is a change to one of the directories along the path to one of the directories you asked to watch. When this flag is set, the event ID is zero and the path corresponds to one of the paths you asked to watch (specifically, the one that changed). The path may no longer exist because it or one of its parents was deleted or renamed. Events with this flag set will only be sent if you passed the flag kFSEvents.CreateFlagWatchRoot to FSEvents.Create when you created the stream. Mount = 64 Denotes a special event sent when a volume is mounted underneath one of the paths being monitored. The path in the event is the path to the newly-mounted volume. You will receive one of these notifications for every volume mount event inside the kernel (independent of DiskArbitration). Beware that a newly-mounted volume could contain an arbitrarily large directory hierarchy. Avoid pitfalls like triggering a recursive scan of a non-local filesystem, which you can detect by checking for the absence of the MNT_LOCAL flag in the f_flags returned by statfs(). Also be aware of the MNT_DONTBROWSE flag that is set for volumes which should not be displayed by user interface elements. Unmount = 128 Denotes a special event sent when a volume is unmounted underneath one of the paths being monitored. The path in the event is the path to the directory from which the volume was unmounted. You will receive one of these notifications for every volume unmount event inside the kernel. This is not a substitute for the notifications provided by the DiskArbitration framework; you only get notified after the unmount has occurred. Beware that unmounting a volume could uncover an arbitrarily large directory hierarchy, although macOS never does that. ItemCreated = 256 A file system object was created at the specific path supplied in this event. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemRemoved = 512 A file system object was removed at the specific path supplied in this event. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemInodeMetaMod = 1024 A file system object at the specific path supplied in this event had its metadata modified. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemRenamed = 2048 A file system object was renamed at the specific path supplied in this event. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemModified = 4096 A file system object at the specific path supplied in this event had its data modified. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemFinderInfoMod = 8192 A file system object at the specific path supplied in this event had its FinderInfo data modified. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemChangeOwner = 16384 A file system object at the specific path supplied in this event had its ownership changed. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemXattrMod = 32768 A file system object at the specific path supplied in this event had its extended attributes modified. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemIsFile = 65536 The file system object at the specific path supplied in this event is a regular file. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemIsDir = 131072 The file system object at the specific path supplied in this event is a directory. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemIsSymlink = 262144 The file system object at the specific path supplied in this event is a symbolic link. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) OwnEvent = 524288 Indicates the event was triggered by the current process. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the MarkSelf flag when creating the stream.) ItemIsHardlink = 1048576 Indicates the object at the specified path supplied in this event is a hard link. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemIsLastHardlink = 2097152 Indicates the object at the specific path supplied in this event was the last hard link. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.) ItemCloned = 4194304 The file system object at the specific path supplied in this event is a clone or was cloned. (This flag is only ever set if you specified the FileEvents flag when creating the stream.)

parameters.name: FSEventsRef
parameters.description: The reference number for this FSEvents object.

functions.name: FSEvents.PathsBeingWatched
functions.description: Queries the paths being watched.

parameters.name: FSEventsRef
parameters.description: The reference number for this FSEvents object.

examples.label: Query paths being watched:
examples.text_code:
Delete All Records [No dialog]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" )]
Set Variable [
$sinceWhen
; Value:"now"]
If [not IsEmpty(
FSEvents::LastID
)]
Set Variable [
$sinceWhen
; Value:
FSEvents::LastID
]
End If
Set Variable [
$fstream
; Value:
MBS
("
FSEvents.Create
";
$path
;
$sinceWhen
; "IgnoreSelf, FileEvents, WatchRoot"; 2)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error"; "Failed to create file system event monitor."]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Paths";
MBS
( "FSEvents.PathsBeingWatched";
$fstream
)]
# ....
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="10" name="Delete All Records">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"now"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sinceWhen</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>not IsEmpty(FSEvents::LastID)</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>FSEvents::LastID</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sinceWhen</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FSEvents.Create"; $path; $sinceWhen; "IgnoreSelf, FileEvents, WatchRoot"; 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fstream</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to create file system event monitor."</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Paths"; MBS( "FSEvents.PathsBeingWatched"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$fstream )</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> ....</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FSEvents.SetScriptName
functions.description: Sets the script name for scheduled script start. The script trigger feature does not work with FileMaker server. Please schedule a script there to check regularly. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: ScriptName

  </Step>

functions.name: FSEvents.Started
functions.description: Queries if FSEvents monitor is started. Returns 1 if started, 0 if stopped.

parameters.name: FSEventsRef
parameters.description: The reference number for this FSEvents object.

functions.name: FSEvents.Stop
functions.description: Stops FSEvent monitor. Unregisters with the FS Events service.  The trigger will not be called for this stream while it is stopped. Stop can only be called if the stream has been started, via FSEvents.Start . Once stopped, the stream can be restarted via FSEvents.Start , at which point it will resume receiving events from where it left off ("sinceWhen").

parameters.name: FSEventsRef
parameters.description: The reference number for this FSEvents object.

functions.name: FSEvents.UUIDForDevice
functions.description: Gets the UUID associated with a device. Returns empty text if not possible (for example, on read-only device). A (non-empty) UUID uniquely identifies a given stream of FSEvents.  If this (non-empty) UUID is different than one that you stored from a previous run then the event stream is different (for example, because FSEvents were purged, because the disk was erased, or because the event ID counter wrapped around back to zero). An empty return value indicates that "historical" events are not available, i.e., you should not supply a "sinceWhen" value to FSEvents.Create other than "now". The UUID is returned as 32 hex characters.

parameters.name: DeviceID
parameters.description: The device ID. Either a number or the path to a file/folder of that device.

examples.label: Queries UUID for root:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FSEvents.UUIDForDevice"; "/" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Llama.Detokenize
functions.signature: MBS( "Llama.Detokenize"; LlamaSession; Tokens { ; remove special; unparse special } )
functions.description: Convert the provided tokens into text (inverse of llama_tokenize()).

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

parameters.name: Tokens
parameters.description: A JSON array with numbers. The token values depend on the model.

parameters.name: remove special
parameters.description: Allow to remove BOS and EOS tokens if model is configured to do so. Pass 1 to allow or 0 to disallow. Default is 1.

parameters.name: unparse special
parameters.description: If true, special tokens are rendered in the output. Pass 1 to allow or 0 to disallow. Default is 1.

functions.name: Llama.InitSampler
functions.description: Initializes the sampler chain. Please pass a JSON array with objects. Each object has an entry "sampler" naming one of the samplers to use. greedy dist seed: optional parameter with seed. top_k k top_p p: e.g. 0.8 min_keep: e.g. 1 min_p p: e.g. 0.8 min_keep: e.g. 1 typical p: e.g. 0.8 min_keep: e.g. 1 temp t: e.g. 0.8 temp_ext t: delta: exponent: top_n_sigma t: xtc p: t: min_keep seed: mirostat n_vocab: seed: optional tau: eta: mirostat_v2 seed: optional tau: eta:

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

parameters.name: Configuration
parameters.description: The JSON array with the sampler objects.

examples.label: Init the sampler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS(	"Llama.InitSampler" ;	$$session ;	JSONSetElement(		"[]" ;		[ "[+].sampler" ; "min_p" ; JSONString ] ;		[ "[:].p" ; ,8 ; JSONNumber ] ;		[ "[:].min_keep" ; 1 ; JSONNumber ] ;		[ "[+].sampler" ; "temp" ; JSONString ] ;		[ "[:].t" ; ,8 ; JSONNumber ] ;		[ "[+].sampler" ; "dist" ; JSONString ]))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Path

functions.name: Llama.LoadLibrary
functions.description: Loads the library. You need to load llama library before you can use it. For Windows you need llama.dll, for macOS you need llama.dylib and for Linux you need llama.so. On Windows you need to load ggml.dll, ggml-base.dll and llama.dll to have all functions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The file path to the library.

examples.label: Loads the library on macOS:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Llama.LoadLibrary"; "/opt/homebrew/lib/libllama.dylib") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]

functions.name: Llama.LoadModel
functions.description: Load the model from a file. If the file is split into multiple parts, the file name must follow this pattern: <name>-%05d-of-%05d.gguf e.g. gemma-3-1b-it.Q8_0.gguf or gpt-oss-20b-Q4_K_M.gguf

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the model.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The JSON with parameters. Optionally you can pass a number for n_gpu_layers or main_gpu. n_gpu_layers: number of layers to store in VRAM, a negative value means all layers

functions.name: Llama.Loaded
functions.description: Whether the library is loaded. Created 10th February 2026 , last changed 10th February 2026 Llama.LoadModel - Llama.ModelChatTemplate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Llama.ModelChatTemplate
functions.description: Queries the chat template text for the model. Returns empty text is template isn't available for the mode.

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

parameters.name: TemplateName
parameters.description: The chat template name. Default is "" to pick the default template.

functions.name: Llama.ModelMetadata
functions.description: Queries the metadata for a model. Returns JSON with keys and values. Created 10th February 2026 , last changed 10th February 2026 Llama.ModelChatTemplate - Llama.ModelSize 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

functions.name: Llama.ModelSize
functions.description: Queries the model size.

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

functions.name: Llama.New
functions.description: Creates a new session. Please call Llama.Release later to release memory.

functions.name: Llama.Query
functions.description: Runs a text through the model. Returns the response back. We tokenize the query, send it to the model and let it answer.

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

parameters.name: Prompt
parameters.description: The prompt text for the chat.

parameters.name: maxToken
parameters.description: The maximum number of tokens allowed before the function cancels. Default is 999.

examples.label: Ask the model something:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("Llama.Query";
$$session
; "What does the German word Sonne mean?")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Llama.Query"; $$session; "What does the German word Sonne mean?")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Llama.Release
functions.description: Releases the session. Closes the sampler, model and context when needed.

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

examples.label: Release if needed in a layout exit script:
examples.text_code:
If [ IsEmpty (
$$session
) ]
# nothing to do
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Llama.Release";
$$session
) ]
Set Variable [
$$session
; Value: "" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ IsEmpty ( $$session ) ]
	# nothing to do
Else
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("Llama.Release"; $$session) ]
	Set Variable [ $$session ; Value: "" ]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Llama.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all sessions.

functions.name: Llama.StartContext

parameters.name: Parameters

functions.name: Llama.Tokenize
functions.signature: MBS( "Llama.Tokenize"; LlamaSession; Text; add special; parse special )
functions.description: Convert the provided text into tokens.

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: add special
parameters.description: Allow to add BOS and EOS tokens if model is configured to do so.

parameters.name: parse special
parameters.description: Allow tokenizing special and/or control tokens which otherwise are not exposed and treated as plaintext. Does not insert a leading space.

examples.label: Tokenize some text:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
tokens =
MBS
("Llama.Tokenize";
$$session
; "Hello World.");
text =
MBS
("
Llama.Detokenize
";
$$session
; tokens)
]; tokens & ¶ & text )
Example result:
[2, 9259, 4109, 236761]
Hello World.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
  tokens = MBS("Llama.Tokenize"; $$session; "Hello World.");
  text = MBS("Llama.Detokenize"; $$session; tokens)
]; tokens & ¶ & text )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Tokenize with and without the BOS token:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
r1 =
MBS
("Llama.Tokenize";
$$session
; "Hello World."; 0; 0);
r2 =
MBS
("Llama.Tokenize";
$$session
; "Hello World."; 1; 1)
]; r1 & ¶ & r2 )
Example result:
[
9259
,
4109
,
236761
]
[
2
,
9259
,
4109
,
236761
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
  r1 = MBS("Llama.Tokenize"; $$session; "Hello World."; 0; 0);
  r2 = MBS("Llama.Tokenize"; $$session; "Hello World."; 1; 1)
]; r1 & ¶ & r2 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Llama.Transcript
functions.description: Queries the transcript. This will apply the stored messages with the chat template to produce the transcript as the LLM would read it. Learn more here: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/wiki/Templates-supported-by-llama_chat_apply_template

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

parameters.name: AddAssistant
parameters.description: Whether to end the prompt with the token(s) that indicate the start of an assistant message. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Default is 1.

parameters.name: TemplateName
parameters.description: The chat template name. Default is "" to pick the default template.

examples.label: Query the transcript:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Llama.Transcript";
$$session
)
Example result:
<start_of_turn>user
Hello<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
Hello there! How's your day going so far? 😊
Is there anything you'd like to chat about, or anything I can help you with?<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Llama.Transcript"; $$session)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Llama.TranscriptJSON
functions.description: Queries the transcript as JSON.

parameters.name: LlamaSession
parameters.description: The session identifier.

functions.name: Menu.AddItem
functions.description: Adds a menu item to the end of the menu. Thus, the receiver does not accept the menu item if it already belongs to another menu. After adding the menu item, the receiver updates itself.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

examples.label: Show a context menu based on a table with menu entries:
examples.text_code:
# go to layout with menu entries
Go to Layout [ “REP” (REP Reports) ]
# this script can be called with various groups
Set Variable [
$type
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
# make a new menu
Set Variable [
$menu
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.CreateMenu
") ]
# loop over records
Go to Record/Request/Page [ First ]
Loop
# if group matches
If [
REP Reports::Group
=
$type
]
# add new menu item with title from table
Set Variable [
$item
; Value:
MBS
( "
MenuItem.CreateMenuItem
";
REP Reports::LabelReport
) ]
# define which script to call if menu item is selected
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MenuItem.SetScriptAction
";
$item
; Get(FileName);
REP Reports::Script
) ]
# add item to menu
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Menu.AddItem";
$menu
;
$item
) ]
End If
# next record?
Go to Record/Request/Page [ Next ; Exit after last ]
End Loop
# switch layout back
Go to Layout [ original layout ]
# Show menu
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.PopUp
";
$menu
; "mouse") ]
# Cleanup memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.Release
";
$menu
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> go to layout with menu entries</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="REP"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> this script can be called with various groups</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$type</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make a new menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.CreateMenu") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$menu</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over records</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> if group matches</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> REP Reports::Group = $type </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add new menu item with title from table</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MenuItem.CreateMenuItem"; REP Reports::LabelReport) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$item</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> define which script to call if menu item is selected</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MenuItem.SetScriptAction"; $item; Get(FileName); REP Reports::Script ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add item to menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.AddItem"; $menu; $item ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next record?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> switch layout back</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="OriginalLayout"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Show menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.PopUp"; $menu; "mouse") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.Release"; $menu) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: QuickMenu custom function:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
/* custom function with parameters: Items */

/* Create a menu */
menu = MBS( "Menu.CreateMenu" );
/* add items from our parameter */
m2 = MBS( "Menu.AddItems"; menu; Items ); 
/* show menu at mouse position */
m3 = MBS( "Menu.PopUp"; menu; "mouse"; 0; 0 );
/* query selected item */
selectedItem = MBS( "Menu.SelectedItem"; menu );
/* if something is selected, query its text */
result = If( Length(selectedItem )>0; MBS( "MenuItem.GetTitle"; selectedItem ); "");
/* Cleanup menu */
m5 = MBS( "Menu.Release"; menu; 1 )

];

/* return selection */
result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: QuickMenu custom function with tags
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
/* custom function with parameters: Items, Tags */

/* Create a menu */
menu = MBS( "Menu.CreateMenu" );
/* add items from our parameter */
m2 = MBS( "Menu.AddItems"; menu; Items; Tags ); 
/* show menu at mouse position */
m3 = MBS( "Menu.PopUp"; menu; "mouse"; 0; 0 );
/* query selected item */
selectedItem = MBS( "Menu.SelectedItem"; menu );
/* if something is selected, query its text */
result = If( Length(selectedItem )>0; MBS( "MenuItem.GetTag"; selectedItem ); "");
/* Cleanup menu */
m5 = MBS( "Menu.Release"; menu; 1 )

];

/* return selected tag */
result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Menu.CreateMenu
functions.description: Creates a menu. You need to later release it with Menu.Release function. Menu reference numbers are starting at 10000 and counting up for each new menu.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title for the menu.

examples.label: Show a context menu based on a table with menu entries:
examples.text_code:
# go to layout with menu entries
Go to Layout [ “REP” (REP Reports) ]
# this script can be called with various groups
Set Variable [
$type
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
# make a new menu
Set Variable [
$menu
; Value:
MBS
( "Menu.CreateMenu") ]
# loop over records
Go to Record/Request/Page [ First ]
Loop
# if group matches
If [
REP Reports::Group
=
$type
]
# add new menu item with title from table
Set Variable [
$item
; Value:
MBS
( "
MenuItem.CreateMenuItem
";
REP Reports::LabelReport
) ]
# define which script to call if menu item is selected
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MenuItem.SetScriptAction
";
$item
; Get(FileName);
REP Reports::Script
) ]
# add item to menu
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.AddItem
";
$menu
;
$item
) ]
End If
# next record?
Go to Record/Request/Page [ Next ; Exit after last ]
End Loop
# switch layout back
Go to Layout [ original layout ]
# Show menu
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.PopUp
";
$menu
; "mouse") ]
# Cleanup memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.Release
";
$menu
) ]

functions.name: Menu.DefineQuickMenu
functions.description: Builds a new menu from a definition text with menu items. This function allows you to easily create a menu with a definition. The first two parameters are required. The "Name" parameter is the name of the menu. The "Definition" parameter is a return-delimited list of menu items for this menu. Each line represents one item on the menu. You can specify a tag value for a menu item by appending a semi-colon and the value onto the end of the item name. For example, if you had "Blue;0000FF" on a line, your menu would have an item with the title "Blue" and when a user selected that item, the tag value "0000FF" could be used for your script. For sub-menus, specify the name of the sub-menu, followed by a greater-than sign (">") followed by the next sub-menu name or menu item. For example, if you had "Colors>Red" on a line, your menu would have a sub-menu named "Colors" with an item inside named "Red". Every sub-menu and menu item can be preceded with one or more special "meta tags" to specify different styles and marks for the sub-menu or item name as explained below:

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The title of the new menu.

parameters.name: Definition
parameters.description: The menu definition.

examples.label: Define a menu:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.DefineQuickMenu"; "ColorsMenu" ; "Red;1¶Blue;2¶Yellow;3" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Define a menu with submenus:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Menu.DefineQuickMenu"; "ColorsMenu"; "Warm colors>Red¶Warm colors>Yellow¶Warm colors>Orange¶Cool colors>Blue¶Cool colors>Green¶Cool colors>Purple")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.DefineQuickMenu"; "ColorsMenu"; "Warm colors>Red¶Warm colors>Yellow¶Warm colors>Orange¶Cool colors>Blue¶Cool colors>Green¶Cool colors>Purple")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Choose from menu with one Let statement:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
// show menu with one let statement

// some list of menu entries
valuelist = "xxx¶yyy¶zzz" ;
// create menu from definition
menu = MBS ( "Menu.DefineQuickmenu"; "x" ; valuelist  ) ;
// show menu near mouse
result = MBS ( "Menu.popup" ; menu ; "mouse" ) ;
// query selected item number
item = IF ( result = 1 ; MBS("Menu.SelectedItem"; menu) ) ;
// query title for that item
title = IF ( Length ( item > 0 ) ; MBS ( "MenuItem.GetTitle" ; item ) ) ;
// release memory
r = MBS ( "Menu.Release"; menu ) ] ; 
// return the selected title
title )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Menu.DefineQuickMenuXML
functions.description: Defines a menu based on a xml description. The xml has a root entry called "menu". Each menu entry can have attributes title, tag, groupID, scriptname, filename and modifierkeys. A menu can have subnodes of name menu or item. The item can have attributes title, tag, scriptname, filename, tag, enabled, selectable, Separator, mark, style, GroupID or modifierkeys. ModifierKeys can include shift, control, option or command. Mark can include mixed, bullet, diamond or checked. Style can include bold, italic or underline. If an item has name "-" or Separator=1 attribute, the plugin will mark it as being a separator. If the root menu entry has a name, we can use that name as menu reference. Added FontColor and BackColor in version 9.4. Added FontName and FontSize in version 13.0 to control the font for a menu item.

parameters.name: XML
parameters.description: The description XML.

examples.label: Defines a quick menu with XML:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Menu.DefineQuickMenuXML"; "<menu name=\"menue\">
<menuitem name=\"Red\" value=\"1\" />
<menuitem name=\"Blue\" value=\"2\" />
<menuitem name=\"Yellow\" value=\"3\" />
</menu>" )
examples.xml_code:

    <menuitem name=\"Red\" value=\"1\" />
    <menuitem name=\"Blue\" value=\"2\" />
    <menuitem name=\"Yellow\" value=\"3\" />
</menu>" )</Calculation>
    </Value>

    </Repetition>

</fmxmlsnippet>

<menuitem name=\"Yellow\" value=\"3\" />
</submenu>

<menuitem name=\"Blue\" value=\"4\" />
<menuitem name=\"Green\" value=\"5\" />
<menuitem name=\"Purple\" value=\"6\" />

</menu>\")" )

</menu>\")" )</Calculation>

functions.name: Menu.GetAllowsContextMenuPlugIns
functions.description: Returns whether the popup menu allows appending of contextual menu plugin items. See also Menu.SetAllowsContextMenuPlugIns .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression to be run when menu is chosen.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetMinimumWidth
functions.description: Returns the minimum width of the menu. Available in OS X v10.6 and later. The minimum width of the menu in screen coordinates. See also Menu.SetMinimumWidth .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetScriptFileName
functions.description: Queries which file contains the script to be run if menu item is selected.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetScriptName
functions.description: Queries the script name of the script to be run if menu item is selected.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetShowsStateColumn
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the receiver displays the state column. Available in OS X v10.5 and later. See also Menu.SetShowsStateColumn .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetTag
functions.description: Queries the tag value of the menu. See also Menu.SetTag . With the tag, we allow you to associate a text with the menu item, for example a record ID.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetTitle
functions.description: Queries the menu title. See also Menu.SetTitle .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.GetURL
functions.description: Queries URL to be triggered for menu action.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.InsertItem
functions.description: Inserts a menu item into the receiver at a specific location.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: An integer index identifying the location of the menu item in the menu.

functions.name: Menu.IsOpen
functions.description: Returns 1 while a menu is open. This allows you to check in some timer script whether the menu is currently open using MBS functions.

functions.name: Menu.ItemAtIndex
functions.description: Queries the ID of the menu item with given index. Index is zero based and ranges from 0 to Menu.NumberOfItems -1.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the menu item to query.

functions.name: Menu.Items
functions.description: Queries the list of IDs for the menu items.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.result: Returns list of IDs or error.

examples.label: Loop over all menuitems in a menu and change font size:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While ( 
[
  // get items in menu 
  items = MBS( "Menu.Items"; $menu ); 
  index = 1;
  count = MBS( "Menu.NumberOfItems"; $menu )
] ; 

// loop till last item
index ≤ count ; 

[ 
  // get the current item
  item = GetValue(items; index);
  // change font size
  r = MBS( "MenuItem.SetFontSize"; item; 16 ); 
  // and continue with next item
  index = index + 1
] ; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Menu.PopUp
functions.signature: MBS( "Menu.PopUp"; menu; Mode { ; X; Y; window; Item; objectName } )
functions.description: Pops up the menu at the specified location. The top left corner of the specified item (if specified, item must be present in the receiver) is positioned at the specified location in the specified view, interpreted in the view's own coordinate system. If you call this on the server for Web Direct, the menu will show on the server, not in the browser. Returns 1 if item was selected or 0 if not. If you like to show a context menu only when user did a right mouse click, you can use the EventMonitor.LastMouseClickButton function to check mouse state.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The mode to use. Can be "mouse", "screen", "object" or "window". Default is mouse for leaving away the parameter or passing empty text. Version 8.3 adds mode "content" to make it relative to the content part of the window, below toolbars.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The relative x coordinates for the position relative to the mouse, screen or window.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The relative y coordinates for the position relative to the mouse, screen or window.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: The window reference for the showing menu relative to a window.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The menu item to be positioned at the specified location in the view. macOS only.

parameters.name: objectName
parameters.description: The object name for mode=object.

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$type</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make a new menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.CreateMenu") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$menu</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over records</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> if group matches</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> REP Reports::Group = $type </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add new menu item with title from table</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MenuItem.CreateMenuItem"; REP Reports::LabelReport) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$item</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> define which script to call if menu item is selected</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MenuItem.SetScriptAction"; $item; Get(FileName); REP Reports::Script ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add item to menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.AddItem"; $menu; $item ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next record?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> switch layout back</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="OriginalLayout"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Show menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.PopUp"; $menu; "mouse") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.Release"; $menu) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Choose from menu with one Let statement:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
// show menu with one let statement

// some list of menu entries
valuelist = "xxx¶yyy¶zzz" ;
// create menu from definition
menu = MBS ( "Menu.DefineQuickmenu"; "x" ; valuelist  ) ;
// show menu near mouse
result = MBS ( "Menu.popup" ; menu ; "mouse" ) ;
// query selected item number
item = IF ( result = 1 ; MBS("Menu.SelectedItem"; menu) ) ;
// query title for that item
title = IF ( Length ( item > 0 ) ; MBS ( "MenuItem.GetTitle" ; item ) ) ;
// release memory
r = MBS ( "Menu.Release"; menu ) ] ; 
// return the selected title
title )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Menu.Release
functions.description: Releases the menu. You need to release menus after you finished using it to avoid running out of memory.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: children
parameters.description: If 1, releases all child menu items and child menus.

functions.name: Menu.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all menus and menu items. You can call this method to release all previously created menus and menu items before you build a new one.

examples.label: Cleanup:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.ReleaseAll" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Menu.RemoveAllItems
functions.description: Removes all the menu items in the receiver. This function is more efficient than removing menu items individually.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.RemoveItem
functions.description: Removes a menu item from the menu. Please use Menu.RemoveItemAtIndex if you have the index and not the reference number.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item to be removed.

functions.name: Menu.RemoveItemAtIndex
functions.description: Removes the menu item at a specified location in the menu. Please use Menu.RemoveItem if you have the reference number and not the index.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: An integer index identifying the menu item. Index must be between 0 and Menu.NumberOfItems -1.

functions.name: Menu.SelectedItem
functions.description: Returns the reference number of the selected menu item.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: Menu.SetAllowsContextMenuPlugIns
functions.description: Sets whether the popup menu allows appending of contextual menu plugin items. Contextual menu plugins are system-wide services provided by other applications. For example, a contextual menu plugin might provide an "Open URL..." service. By enabling context menu plugins your application's contextual menu will display the appropriate items for the currently selected data type. See also Menu.GetAllowsContextMenuPlugIns .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: 1 if the popup menu should allow context menu plugin items to be appending, otherwise 0.

functions.name: Menu.SetEvaluate
functions.description: Sets expression to be run when menu is chosen. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the hotkey reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: Menu.SetMinimumWidth
functions.description: Set the minimum width of the menu. The menu will not draw smaller than its minimum width, but may draw larger if it needs more space. The default value is 0. See also Menu.GetMinimumWidth .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The minimum width of the menu in screen coordinates.

functions.name: Menu.SetScriptAction
functions.description: Sets which script is called if a menu item in this menu is selected. You can use MenuItem.SetScriptAction to associate a script with individual items or use this function for a whole menu. See also Menu.GetScriptFileName and Menu.GetScriptName . The script parameter is the MenuItem reference, so you can use functions like MenuItem.GetTitle to get the title or MenuItem.GetTag to get an associated value. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename of file which contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script.

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: If you want a specific script parameter. Default is to leave empty and get the ID fo the menu item.

functions.name: Menu.SetShowsStateColumn
functions.description: Sets whether the receiver displays the state column. Available in OS X v10.5 and later. See also Menu.GetShowsStateColumn .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Pass 1 to show or 0 to hide the state column.

functions.name: Menu.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag of the menu. See also Menu.GetTag . With the tag, we allow you to associate a text with the menu item, for example a record ID.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: Menu.SetTitle
functions.description: Sets the menu title. See also Menu.GetTitle .

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new title.

functions.name: Menu.SetURL
functions.description: Sets the URL to query when menu is used. $$ID$$ in the URL is replaced with menu ID. Queries http/ftp/sftp URLs with CURL, other URLs passed to OS to open.

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL to trigger.

functions.name: NetworkInterfaces.Count
functions.description: Queries number of network interfaces found. If called first without a call to NetworkInterfaces.Update , the plugin will internally call NetworkInterfaces.Update .

functions.name: NetworkInterfaces.Default
functions.description: Queries default network interface. Can be used to find IP, name or MAC of the default interface. Default is the first connected interface.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: What value to return. Default is name. Mode can be IP, IPv4, IPv6, Index, InterfaceIndex, Name or MAC.

examples.label: Queries name of default network interface:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "NetworkInterfaces.Default"; "Name" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: NetworkInterfaces.JSON
functions.description: Returns all network interfaces as JSON. If called first without a call to NetworkInterfaces.Update , the plugin will internally call NetworkInterfaces.Update . One of the interfaces may be Wifi or a VPN. You can check if the interface of is active and has an IP address.

functions.name: NetworkInterfaces.Update
functions.description: Queries current network interfaces. Normally you call NetworkInterfaces.Update function. Then you call NetworkInterfaces.Count to query number of network interfaces and finally for each value the NetworkInterfaces.Value function.

functions.name: NetworkInterfaces.Value
functions.description: Queries the

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index from 0 to NetworkInterfaces.Count -1.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to query: Name, IPv4, IPv6, IP, MAC, Destination, Broadcast, Netmask, NetmaskIPv4, NetmaskIPv6, InterfaceIndex, Multicast, HasBroadcast, HasDestination, Up, Loopback, IPv4s, IPv6s, IPv4Count or IPv6Count.

functions.name: PList.ReadData
functions.description: Read property list from data. Pass in either text with XML or container with plist file, which may be XML or binary format. Since JSON doesn't have separate data types for date or blocks of bytes, this is converted to text. Data is base64 encoded. Since we can't preserve all details, reading and writing again is a loosely conversion.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: Pass text with XML or container with plist file.

examples.label: Read plist:
examples.text_code:
MBS

examples.label: Read text from a text clipping:
examples.text_code:
Let([
containerValue =
Kontakte::Foto
;
plist =
MBS
( "PList.ReadData"; containerValue );
rtf = JSONGetElement ( plist; "['UTI-Data'].['public.rtf']");
text =
MBS
( "
Text.RTFToText
"; rtf)
]; text)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  containerValue = Kontakte::Foto;
  plist = MBS( "PList.ReadData"; containerValue );
  rtf = JSONGetElement ( plist; "['UTI-Data'].['public.rtf']");
  text = MBS( "Text.RTFToText"; rtf)
]; text)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PList.ReadFile
functions.description: Reads property list files. Can read files in binary or XML format. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the plist file.

examples.label: Read FileMaker info.plist file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PList.ReadFile"; "/Applications/FileMaker Pro.app/Contents/Info.plist")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PList.WriteData
functions.description: Encodes property list data. Takes JSON and produces matching property list in XML format. You can write this into a plist file with Text.WriteTextFile in UTF-8 encoding. You can use PList.ReadData to convert back from plist to JSON.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The data with the JSON data as input.

examples.label: Convert JSON to Plist:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "PList.WriteData"; "{\"id\":1234}" )
Example result:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
<key>id</key>
<integer>1234</integer>
</dict>
</plist>
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PList.WriteData"; "{\"id\":1234}" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.BuildNumber
functions.description: Queries the build number of the plugin. Sometimes you may have two plugins with same version number, but one is newer as it has a higher build number. Version 5.0 returns 1139.

examples.label: Show build number:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BuildNumber" )</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.CallCounter
functions.description: Queries call counter. The MBS Plugin counts how often it is called and this function returns the current value. Calling this function increments the value.

functions.name: Plugin.CallErrorScriptTrigger
functions.description: Triggers error script. To test your script trigger for error, you can call this method to test.

functions.name: Plugin.CompileDate
functions.description: Queries compile date as FileMaker date. Allows you to compare current time to compile date to check how old the plugin is. See also CompileDate and CompileTime .

examples.label: Check age of plugin:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(CurrentDate) - MBS( "Plugin.CompileDate" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Check and ask user to install newer version than the prerelease they have:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("
Plugin.IsPrerelease
") ]
If [ Get(CurrentDate) -
MBS
( "Plugin.
CompileDate
" ) >= 14 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Update" ; "Please install newer version of
MBS
Plugin." & ¶ &
MBS
("Version") ]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS("Plugin.IsPrerelease") ] 
	If [ Get(CurrentDate) - MBS( "Plugin.CompileDate" ) >= 14 ] 
		Show Custom Dialog [ "Update" ; "Please install newer version of MBS Plugin." & ¶ & MBS("Version") ] 
	End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.CustomFunctionParameter
functions.description: Queries custom function parameter with given index. If index is out of range, we return empty result. Please avoid recursion in calling a plugin defined custom function within a plugin defined custom function since we can't know which parameters belong to which call.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The zero based index of the parameter to query. Range from 0 to Plugin.CustomFunctionParameterCount -1.

functions.name: Plugin.CustomFunctionParameterCount
functions.description: Queries number of parameters to the current custom function.

functions.name: Plugin.CustomFunctions
functions.description: Queries details on all registered custom functions. Returns a JSON with the following values: id name type: Function or ScriptStep. engine: FileMaker, WebKit or JavaScript. globals: true or false for whether it uses globals in JavaScript. prototype

examples.label: Query custom functions:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.CustomFunctions" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.DisableFunction
functions.description: Disables a plugin function. This can be useful with security when you want to be sure your script does not use certain methods. The plugin removes the function from the global function list so it can't be called until you restart FileMaker.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A function name like RemoteControl.ClickMouse .

examples.label: Disable a function:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.DisableFunction"; "RemoteControl.ClickMouse" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.FunctionCount
functions.description: Queries number of functions available. As some functions are not available, this number is usually lower than our total number. e.g. Windows only functions are not included on Mac count.

functions.name: Plugin.Functions
functions.description: Returns a list of all the functions in the plugin. The list is separated with commas and spaces.

examples.label: Query the functions in the plugins:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.Functions")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.GetFunctionParameters
functions.description: Queries the function metadata for parameter list. The returned list describes the parameter list after the initial function name.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A function name like RemoteControl.ClickMouse .

examples.label: Query parameter for Register function:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("Plugin.GetFunctionParameters"; "Register")
Example result:
Licensee Name
Component
License Type
Expire Month
Serial number
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.GetFunctionParameters"; "Register")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.GetFunctionPlatforms
functions.description: Queries the function metadata for platform support. The text returned contains letters WMLI to indicate that the function is available or Windows, MacOS, Linux or iOS. A function returning e.g. "MI" will work on macOS and iOS, but not on Windows and Linux.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A function name like RemoteControl.ClickMouse .

examples.label: Check TAPI function:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.GetFunctionPlatforms"; "TAPI.Release" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.GetSuccessReturn
functions.description: Queries current OK return.

functions.name: Plugin.InstallSignalHandlers
functions.description: Installs signal handlers. When something happens like a segmentation fault (a crash in C code), the plugin can print the backtrace (list of functions called) to help to debug the problem. Usually you call this function only if we ask you to help on debugging a crash on Linux or MacOS.

functions.name: Plugin.IsLicenseKeyHidden
functions.description: Queries whether to hide license key. If true, we hide license key in preferences dialog.

functions.name: Plugin.IsMaintenanceExpired
functions.description: Queries whether maintenance is expired. This basically compares the end date for the license key to today’s day.

examples.label: Check it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>mbs("Plugin.IsMaintenanceExpired") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$expired</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Remind to update plugin:
examples.text_code:
If [ GetAsNumber(Middle(
MBS
( "CompileDate" );7;6)) < Year ( Get(CurrentDate )) and Month ( Get(CurrentDate )) > 3 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Your
MBS
Plugin could be updated”. ; "It's spring and you use plugin from last year?" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ GetAsNumber(Middle(MBS( "CompileDate" );7;6)) < Year ( Get(CurrentDate )) and Month ( Get(CurrentDate )) > 3 ] 
	Show Custom Dialog [ "Your MBS Plugin could be updated”. ; "It's spring and you use plugin from last year?" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.JasperVersion
