functions.name: Plugin.LicenseeName
functions.description: Queries licensee name. Returns the name used in Register function.

examples.label: Script to report back status of MBS Plugin:
examples.text_code:
# Script to trigger somewhere to report back details about
MBS
Plugin
Set Variable [
$version
; Value: GetAsText(
MBS
("Version")) ]
Set Variable [
$platform
; Value:
MBS
( "Platform" ) ]
Set Variable [
$buildNumber
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.BuildNumber
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.Path
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$IsRegistered
; Value:
MBS
( "IsRegistered" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Name
; Value:
MBS
( "Plugin.LicenseeName" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Debug
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.isDebug
" ) ]
If [
$version
= "?" ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value: "
MBS
Plugin not installed. " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ]
Else
Set Variable [
$l1
; Value: "
MBS
Plugin " &
$version
& ", build " &
$buildNumber
& " installed in " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ]
Set Variable [
$l2
; Value: "¶Platform: " &
$Platform
& "¶Path: " &
$path
]
Set Variable [
$l3
; Value: If(
$
IsRegistered
; "¶Licensed for: " &
$Name
; "¶No license set.") ]
Set Variable [
$l4
; Value: "¶Debug: " & If(
$debug
; "yes"; "no") ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
$l1
&
$l2
&
$l3
]
End If
Exit Script [ Text Result:
$text
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Script to trigger somewhere to report back details about MBS Plugin</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText(MBS("Version")) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$version</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Platform" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$platform</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.BuildNumber" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$buildNumber</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.Path" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "IsRegistered" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$IsRegistered</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LicenseeName" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.isDebug" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Debug</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $version = "?" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"MBS Plugin not installed. " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"MBS Plugin " & $version & ", build " & $buildNumber & " installed in " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"¶Platform: " & $Platform & "¶Path: " & $path </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If($IsRegistered; "¶Licensed for: " & $Name ; "¶No license set.") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"¶Debug: " & If($debug; "yes"; "no") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l4</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$l1 & $l2 & $l3 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: $text </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.LimitFunction
functions.signature: MBS( "Plugin.LimitFunction"; Name { ; privilegeSets; AccountNames; ScriptNames; FileNames } )
functions.description: Limits a function. Once limited, the function can only be called if current privilege set, account name and script name match those in the list. Lists can be empty if not needed. For FileMaker hosting of several clients with one server, we highly recommend to limit the available plugin functions via Plugin.SetFunctions function. You also should limit some functions like Files.Delete or System.Shutdown to be only called by certain user accounts and/or script.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A function name like RemoteControl.ClickMouse .

parameters.name: privilegeSets
parameters.description: List of privilege set names.

parameters.name: AccountNames
parameters.description: List of account names.

parameters.name: ScriptNames
parameters.description: List of script names. Each entry contains the name fo the file followed with ":" and the script name.

parameters.name: FileNames
parameters.description: List of file names allowed. Must be same name as returned by Get(FileName).

examples.label: Limit a function to be called only from a certain script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.LimitFunction"; "Version"; ""; ""; "test.fmp12:test")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Limit a function to be called only from a certain account name:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.LimitFunction"; "Text.WriteTextFile"; "Backup"; ""; "")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Limit Shell.Execute to Exports file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LimitFunction"; "Shell.Execute"; ""; ""; ""; "Exports" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.LimitToFile
functions.description: Sets which files are allowed to use our plugin. This limitation is enforced early, so you can't even query the plugin version anymore if not allowed. Please use Plugin.SetFunctions later to disallow calling this function to avoid someone changing the allowed file list. The setting is not permanent, so please do this in start script of the solution.

parameters.name: FileList
parameters.description: The list of file names to allow. We use Get(FileName) and compare case sensitive, so please match the names exactly.

examples.label: Limit to current file only:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LimitToFile"; Get(FileName) ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Limit to a few files in a solution:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LimitToFile"; "MyInvoices¶MyContacts¶MyAssets") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Allow all files:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LimitToFile"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.LoadIconvLibrary
functions.description: Loads the iconv library. Some plugin parts like Archive functions can make use of libiconv for text encoding conversion. If you explicitly need, you can load the library on start of solution. MBS Plugin may try to load iconv.dll/dylib/so automatically when first iconv function is called. You can find a precompiled Windows library here: https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/files/Libs/

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the library.

examples.label: Load library on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LoadIconvLibrary"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\libiconv2.dll" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.LockFunction

parameters.name: Name

parameters.name: Password

functions.name: Plugin.ObjectCounts
functions.description: Returns JSON with object counts. If you wonder what is using up your memory, you can ask if the plugin has objects laying around, that you may have forgotten to release. The keys are different on macOS, Windows, Linux and iOS since some features don't exist on all platforms.

parameters.name: SkipZeros
parameters.description: Whether to skip zero values. Default is false. Pass 1 to skip.

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.PNGVersion
functions.description: Queries PNG version. This function is to allow you an automatic check of the PNG library version. For 7.2 it is 1.6.14 and for 7.3 1.6.30.

functions.name: Plugin.Path
functions.description: Queries the native file path to the plugin. The file path includes the file name of the plugin. On Linux this returns a path like "/opt/FileMaker/FileMaker Server/Database Server/Extensions/MBS.fmx". On MacOS this points usually to the application folder, e.g. "/Applications/FileMaker Pro 16 Advanced/Extensions/MBS.fmplugin"

examples.label: Write DynaPDF library to same folder as plugin:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [ “
MBS
Update” (
MBS
Update) ; Animation: None ]
Go to Record/Query/Page [ First ]
Set Variable [
$folder
; Value:
MBS
( "Plugin.Path" ) ]
Set Variable [
$folder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.RemoveLastPathComponent
";
$folder
) ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$folder
; "dynapdf.dylib" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Plat
; Value:
MBS
("
Files.MoveToTrash
";
$path
) ]
Set Variable [
$Plat
; Value:
MBS
("
Files.WriteFile
";
MBS
Update::dynaPdfMac
;
$path
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="MBS Update"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.Path" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.RemoveLastPathComponent"; $folder ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $folder; "dynapdf.dylib" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.MoveTotrash"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Plat</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.WriteFile"; MBS Update::dynaPdfMac; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Plat</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.RegisterFunction
functions.signature: MBS( "Plugin.RegisterFunction"; functionId; Name; Prototype; Expression { ; MinParameter; MaxParameter; Flags } )
functions.description: Registers a custom function with FileMaker or JavaScript code. Allows you to define custom functions for your solution, which are accessible from all files. Please register the same function each time your solution opens with the same IDs. FileMaker usually finds functions by ID, but with Evaluate() it matches them by name. If you take variable number of arguments, you need to use Plugin.CustomFunctionParameter function.

parameters.name: functionId
parameters.description: The function ID. Must be unique and 3 or higher. MBS function is ID 1 and MBS script step is ID 2.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the custom function.

parameters.name: Prototype
parameters.description: The prototype to show in the user interface. Defines the names of parameters.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to run.

parameters.name: MinParameter
parameters.description: The minimum parameter count.

parameters.name: MaxParameter
parameters.description: The maximum parameter count. Pass -1 for unlimited parameters.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the function.

examples.label: Register custom function:
examples.text_code:
# Register one function. Replacing older one with same id
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Plugin.RegisterFunction";
123; // ID to use
"Concat"
"Concat(Value1; Value2)";
"Value1 & Value2";
2; // min and max parameters
2) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to add function." ;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Register one function. Replacing older one with same id</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.RegisterFunction";	123; // ID to use	"Concat"	"Concat(Value1; Value2)"; 	"Value1 & Value2"; 	2; // min and max parameters	2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to add function." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Register a function with JavaScript:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.RegisterFunction"; 7; "ConcatJS"; "ConcatJS(Value1; Value2)"; "function ConcatJS(a, b) {
  return String(a) + String(b);
}"; 2; 2; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Register a new counter function using WebKit with globals:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.RegisterFunction"; 11; "TestCounterWK()"; "function TestCounterWK() {
  globalThis.counter = (globalThis.counter || 0) + 1;
  return globalThis.counter;
}"; 0; 0; 2 + 4)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.RegisterScriptStep
functions.signature: MBS( "Plugin.RegisterScriptStep"; Script Step ID; Name; Definition; Expression; Description { ; Flags } )
functions.description: Registers a script step dynamically. Allows you to define custom script steps for your solution. Please register the same script step each time your solution opens with the same IDs. FileMaker usually finds script steps by ID. See also Plugin.RegisterFunction for registering a function.

parameters.name: Script Step ID
parameters.description: The script step ID. Must be unique and 3 or higher. MBS function is ID 1 and MBS script step is ID 2.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the custom function.

parameters.name: Definition
parameters.description: An XML to define the script step.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to run.

parameters.name: Description
parameters.description: The description to show in the user interface.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the function.

examples.label: Register a script step:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.RegisterScriptStep"; 
// internal ID. Must be unique 
123; 
// name of script step
"Concat"; 
// XML definition of parameters
"<PluginStep>
	<Parameter Type=\"target\" Label=\"Destination\" ShowInLine=\"true\"/>
	<Parameter Type=\"calc\" DataType=\"text\" ShowInline=\"true\"  Label=\"FirstName\"  ID=\"0\"/>
	<Parameter Type=\"calc\" DataType=\"text\" ShowInline=\"true\"  Label=\"SecondName\" ID=\"1\"/>
</PluginStep>";
// calculation 
"MBS(\"Plugin.ScriptStepParameter\"; 0) & MBS(\"Plugin.ScriptStepParameter\"; 1)"; 
// description
"Concat two texts" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.ScriptStepParameter
functions.description: Queries script step parameter with given index. If index is out of range, we return empty result.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The zero based index of the parameter to query. Range from 0 to Plugin.ScriptStepParameterCount -1.

functions.name: Plugin.ScriptStepParameterCount
functions.description: Queries number of parameters to the current script step.

functions.name: Plugin.Seats
functions.description: Queries the internal seats value.

examples.label: Display seat count:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.Seats")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.SetErrorScriptTrigger
functions.description: Sets script to trigger if plugin call returns an error. You can call Plugin.CallErrorScriptTrigger to test this. The script is called with JSON block as parameter and this JSON includes entries for Function, Parameters, Result, UserName, FileName, ScriptName, AccountName, HostName, RecordID, CurrentTimeStamp, LayoutTableName, LayoutName, WindowName and TraceID. Please note that this function may trigger scripts at unexpected points, e.g. when a calculation in a layout causes an error. See also ErrorLog function. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

functions.name: Plugin.SetFunctions
functions.description: Sets the function list the plugin has. By default plugin allows use of 3000 and more functions. You can use this command to limit what functions are available to your solution. This avoids some calling a function you don't want to have, e.g. not allow Files.Delete call. For FileMaker hosting of several clients with one server, we highly recommend to limit the available plugin functions via Plugin.SetFunctions function. You also should limit some functions like Files.Delete or System.Shutdown to be only called by certain user accounts and/or script. See Plugin.LimitFunction for this. Version 11.5 allows wildcards, e.g. CURL.* to include all CURL functions.

parameters.name: FunctionList
parameters.description: The new function list.

examples.label: Allow only two functions:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.SetFunctions"; "Version¶Functions" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: A typical MBS Init script for server usage:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("IsRegistered") ≠ 1]
If [
MBS
("IsServer")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Server"; 123; 123)]
# limit to the commands you need
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "Plugin.SetFunctions"; "
IsRegistered
¶
CURL.New
¶
CURL.Perform
..." )]
# enable trace for errors
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
Trace.SetErrorsOnly
"; 1 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "Trace"; "/Library/FileMaker Server/Logs/
MBS
.txt" ) ]
Else
# register on client
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "5 seats"; 123; 123)]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS("IsRegistered") ≠ 1]
	If [MBS("IsServer")]
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("Register"; "test"; "Server"; 123; 123)]
		# limit to the commands you need
		Set Variable [$r; MBS( "Plugin.SetFunctions"; "IsRegistered¶CURL.New¶CURL.Perform..." )]
		# enable trace for errors
		Set Variable [$r; MBS( "Trace.ErrorsOnly"; 1 ) ]
		Set Variable [$r; MBS( "Trace"; "/Library/FileMaker Server/Logs/MBS.txt" ) ]
	Else
		# register on client
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("Register"; "test"; "5 seats"; 123; 123)]
	End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Use wildcards:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.SetFunctions"; "Version¶Functions¶CURL.*¶Plugin.*" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.description: Pass 1 to hide license key or 0 to show it.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.SetPreviewSize
functions.description: Sets which size plugin uses for preview images. When plugin returns PDF or Image, we add JPEG preview. e.g. in DynaPDF, PDFKit and GraphicsMagick functions. With version 11.5 or later, you can set this to -1 to disable preview generation.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: The size for width and height to use for generating previews. Default is 600.

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.SetSuccessReturn
functions.description: Sets what the plugin returns on success. Default is "OK", but this can be changed here to some other text or number. Some users prefer 1 or "success" as result.

functions.name: Plugin.StartTimeStamp
functions.description: Queries time the plugin was loaded.

functions.name: Plugin.State
functions.description: Queries plugin state. Result may be one of: Trial for a trial license. Free for the free tier when no function was used that needs a license. Licensed if a valid license is put in. Expired if a valid license is put in, but the maintenance is expired. Demo if no license is set and you used functions, that require one. Dead if demo mode was run too long.
functions.result: Returns Trial, Free, Licensed, Expired, Demo, Dead or an error.

examples.label: Check expiration state:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "Plugin.State" ) = "Expired" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "
MBS
license maintenance expire…" ; "please update the license." ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "Plugin.State" ) = "Expired" ] 
	Show Custom Dialog [ "MBS license maintenance expire…" ; "please update the license." ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Check state:
examples.text_code:
# New Script in file Assets
If [
MBS
("Plugin.State") = "Free" ]
# you may need to use some more
Else If [
MBS
("Plugin.State") = "Licensed" ]
# you have a license
Else If [
MBS
("Plugin.State") = "Trial" ]
# you have a trial license
Else
# you may want to order something?
Open URL [ With dialog: Off ; "https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> New Script in file Assets</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("Plugin.State") = "Free" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> you may need to use some more</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("Plugin.State") = "Licensed" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> you have a license</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("Plugin.State") = "Trial" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> you have a trial license</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> you may want to order something?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="111" name="Open URL">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <DisableStepCollapsed state="False"/>
    <Calculation>"https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.UnregisterFunction
functions.description: Unregister a function. If passed no parameters, we remove all functions. If you pass one parameter, we check for the function ID to match. And if you pass two parameters, we check for both name and function ID to match.

parameters.name: functionId
parameters.description: The function ID. Must be unique and 3 or higher. MBS function is ID 1 and MBS script step is ID 2.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the custom function.

examples.label: Unregister all custom functions:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.UnregisterFunction")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Unregister function 8:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.UnregisterFunction"; 8)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.UnregisterScriptStep
functions.description: Unregister a script step. If passed no parameters, we remove all script steps. If you pass one parameter, we check for the script step ID to match. And if you pass two parameters, we check for both name and script step ID to match.

parameters.name: Script Step ID
parameters.description: The script step ID. Must be unique and 3 or higher. MBS function is ID 1 and MBS script step is ID 2.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the custom function.

functions.name: Plugin.UsedFunctionCounts
functions.description: Queries plugin function usage counts. Returns a JSON array with function names and their call counts.

functions.name: Plugin.UsedFunctions
functions.description: Queries used functions. Can be useful to check what's needed and use with Plugin.SetFunctions .

functions.name: Plugin.WebPVersion
functions.description: Returns version of WebP library. Should be a version string like "1.3.0".

examples.label: Show version:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "WebP Library Version" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.WebPVersion" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Plugin.ZLibVersion
functions.description: Queries zlib version number. Currently reports 1.2.8.

functions.name: Plugin.isDebug
functions.description: Checks whether this is debug version of MBS Plugin. The debug version is available on request and given to users to find the roots of a bug. Always returns 0 since we stopped building separate debug copies starting with version 9.5.

examples.label: Query debug status
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Plugin.IsDebug")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

MBS
Plugin
Set Variable [
$version
; Value: GetAsText(
MBS
("Version")) ]
Set Variable [
$platform
; Value:
MBS
( "Platform" ) ]

; Value:
MBS
( "

" ) ]

Plugin not installed. " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ]

Plugin " &

Set Variable [
$l2
; Value: "¶Platform: " &

& "¶Path: " &

]
Set Variable [
$l3
; Value: If(
$
IsRegistered
; "¶Licensed for: " &
$Name
; "¶No license set.") ]
Set Variable [

; Value: "¶Debug: " & If(
$debug
; "yes"; "no") ]

&

]

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Script to trigger somewhere to report back details about MBS Plugin</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText(MBS("Version")) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$version</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Platform" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$platform</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.BuildNumber" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$buildNumber</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.Path" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "IsRegistered" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$IsRegistered</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LicenseeName" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.isDebug" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Debug</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $version = "?" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"MBS Plugin not installed. " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"MBS Plugin " & $version & ", build " & $buildNumber & " installed in " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"¶Platform: " & $Platform & "¶Path: " & $path </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If($IsRegistered; "¶Licensed for: " & $Name ; "¶No license set.") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"¶Debug: " & If($debug; "yes"; "no") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l4</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$l1 & $l2 & $l3 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: $text </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Screen.DockHeight
functions.description: Queries dock height. The plugin checks screen height compared to usable screen height, which gives the dock height on the main screen.

examples.label: Query dock size:
examples.text_code:
Show Custom Dialog [ "Dock Size" ;
MBS
( "
Screen.DockWidth
" ) & " x " &
MBS
( "Screen.DockHeight" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Dock Size" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Screen.DockWidth" ) & " x " & MBS( "Screen.DockHeight" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Screen.Height
functions.description: Queries screen height. FileMaker's built in functions for returning related information only works for the "desktop" and may include more than one screen in a Multiple monitor situation. Also Get(WindowDesktopHeight) returns the height of the desktop not the height of the monitor. The desktop height does not include the height of the menu. For version 7.1 we added support for multiple monitors for Windows.

parameters.name: ScreenID
parameters.description: The zero based index of the screen to to target.

parameters.name: Unit
parameters.description: Can be point or pixel or empty. Default is pixel on Windows and point on macOS/iOS.

examples.label: Get the first screen's co-ordinates
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "
Screen.Left
" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS
( "
Screen.Top
" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS
( "
Screen.Width
" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS
( "Screen.Height" ; 0 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Screen.Left" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS( "Screen.Top" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS( "Screen.Width" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS( "Screen.Height" ; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

("Screen.Height"; 0; "pixel") & ¶ &

("

"; 1; "point") & "/" &

("

MBS
("

"; 1; "point") & "x" &
MBS
("Screen.Height"; 1; "point") & ¶ &
MBS
("
Screen.Left
"; 1; "pixel") & "/" &
MBS
("
Screen.Top
"; 1; "pixel") & " " &

("
Screen.Width
"; 1; "pixel") & "x" &
MBS
("Screen.Height"; 1; "pixel")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Screen.Left"; 0; "point") & "/" & MBS("Screen.Top"; 0; "point") & " " &
MBS("Screen.Width"; 0; "point") & "x" & MBS("Screen.Height"; 0; "point") & ¶ &

MBS("Screen.Left"; 0; "pixel") & "/" & MBS("Screen.Top"; 0; "pixel") & " " &
MBS("Screen.Width"; 0; "pixel") & "x" & MBS("Screen.Height"; 0; "pixel") & ¶ &

MBS("Screen.Left"; 1; "point") & "/" & MBS("Screen.Top"; 1; "point") & " " &
MBS("Screen.Width"; 1; "point") & "x" & MBS("Screen.Height"; 1; "point") & ¶ &

MBS("Screen.Left"; 1; "pixel") & "/" & MBS("Screen.Top"; 1; "pixel") & " " &
MBS("Screen.Width"; 1; "pixel") & "x" & MBS("Screen.Height"; 1; "pixel")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: ScreenID
parameters.description: The zero based index of the screen to to target.

( "Screen.Left" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS
( "
Screen.Top

MBS
( "
Screen.Width
" ; 0 ) & "' " &
MBS
( "
Screen.Height
" ; 0 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

examples.label: Figure out on which screen the first window is:
examples.text_code:
Let([
WindowCenterX =
MBS
( "
Window.GetLeft
"; 0 ) +
MBS
( "
Window.GetWidth
"; 0 );
WindowCenterY =
MBS
( "
Window.GetTop
"; 0 ) +
MBS
( "
Window.GetHeight
"; 0 );
$ScreenIndex
= -1;
r = While (
[
c =
MBS
( "
Screen.Count
" );
i = 0
] ;
i < c ;
[
l =
MBS
( "Screen.Left"; i );
t =
MBS
( "
Screen.Top
"; i );
w =
MBS
( "
Screen.Width
"; i );
h =
MBS
( "
Screen.Height
"; i );
$ScreenIndex
= If(
(l ≤ WindowCenterX) AND
(t ≤ WindowCenterY) AND
(WindowCenterX ≤ l + w) AND
(WindowCenterY ≤ t + h) ; i;
$ScreenIndex
);
i = i + 1 ] ; 0 )
];
$ScreenIndex
)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
	WindowCenterX = MBS( "Window.GetLeft"; 0 ) + MBS( "Window.GetWidth"; 0 );
	WindowCenterY = MBS( "Window.GetTop"; 0 ) + MBS( "Window.GetHeight"; 0 );
	$ScreenIndex = -1;

	r = While ( 
	[ 
		c = MBS( "Screen.Count" ); 
		i = 0 
	] ; 
	i < c ; 
	[ 
		l = MBS( "Screen.Left"; i );
		t = MBS( "Screen.Top"; i );
		w = MBS( "Screen.Width"; i );
		h = MBS( "Screen.Height"; i );
		$ScreenIndex = If( 
			(l ≤ WindowCenterX) AND 
			(t ≤ WindowCenterY) AND 
			(WindowCenterX ≤ l + w) AND 
			(WindowCenterY ≤ t + h) ; i; $ScreenIndex ); 
		i = i + 1 ] ; 0 )

]; $ScreenIndex)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Screen.Scale
functions.description: Queries scale of screen. Normal screens have 1, retina screens have 2 and on iPhone some have even 3. Windows has same scale factor for all screens, which can be fractions like 1.5.

parameters.name: Screen ID
parameters.description: The zero based index of the screen to to target. Default is main screen.

functions.description: Queries screen position. Returns the Top coordinate of the screen indicated by the Screen ID parameter Also Get(WindowDesktopHeight) returns the height of the desktop not the height of the monitor. The desktop height does not include the height of the menu. For version 7.1 we added support for multiple monitors for Windows.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.BringToFront
functions.description: Brings the Script Workspace window to front.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.CopyScriptText
functions.description: Copies current script in Script Workspace to clipboard. Includes colors for script steps. Maybe useful for automation workflows where you like to evaluate an expression by hotkey which puts current script on clipboard and then pastes it in your CMS for the website.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.CurrentTab
functions.description: Queries current tab title.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.ExpandScriptFolders
functions.description: Expands all scripts in the Script Workspace. Returns number of folders. This function checks for a license. Created 14th August 2017 , last changed 16th November 2023 ScriptWorkspace.CurrentTab - ScriptWorkspace.GetBackgroundColor 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.GetBackgroundColor
functions.description: Queries the current color of the script workspace background.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.GetScriptListSearch
functions.description: Queries search field in script list search.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.IsActive
functions.description: Checks if front window with focus is Script Workspace. Returns 1 if Script Workspace has focus. e.g. if you trigger an evaluation via hotkey functions, you can use this function to check if you are in Script Workspace.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.IsFocusOnScriptList
functions.description: Queries whether the focus is to the script list in the Script Workspace. Returns 1 if focus in the Script Worksapce is on the script list.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.IsVisible
functions.description: Checks whether Script Workspace is visible. Returns 1 if visible or 0 if not.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.LineCount
functions.description: Queries line count of current script.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.OpenScript
functions.description: Opens a script by name. The script list does not need to be visible.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to select. Must be visible in the left list of scripts in Script Workspace.

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: The group the script is inside. If the script is not found directly, we look for the group to open it, then search again.

examples.label: Open a script in a second:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay
"; 1; "
MBS
( \"ScriptWorkspace.OpenScript\"; \"Test Script\" )" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay"; 1; "MBS( \"ScriptWorkspace.OpenScript\"; \"Test Script\" )" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.ScriptNames
functions.description: Queries list of script names in Script Workspace. See also FM.ScriptNames to ask FileMaker instead of looking into the window. This function includes folder names.

parameters.name: SelectedOnly
parameters.description: Pass 1 to only get selected items.

examples.label: Query names and paths:
examples.text_code:
# List of the selected script names
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "ScriptWorkspace.ScriptNames"; 1) ]
# List of the selected script paths in format Folder→Folder→Script
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
ScriptWorkspace.ScriptPaths
"; 1) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> List of the selected script names</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.ScriptNames"; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> List of the selected script paths in format Folder→Folder→Script</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.ScriptPaths"; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.ScriptText
functions.description: Copies text of current script. Same as copy button in Script Workspace.

parameters.name: SelectedOnly
parameters.description: Return only selected text lines. Default is 0.

parameters.name: EndOfLine
parameters.description: The character for end of line within script steps, e.g. multi line comments. If not specified or empty, defaults to Char(13).

examples.label: Query text with double <CR> for delimiter within comments:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ScriptWorkspace.ScriptText"; 0; "<CR>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.ScriptsVisible

( "
ScriptWorkspace.StepsVisible

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.ScrollToLine

parameters.name: Line

examples.label: Scrolls to last line:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.ScrollToLine"; -1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.ScrollToSelection

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SelectGroup

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: The group name.

parameters.name: Expand
parameters.description: Whether to expand the item. Pass 1 to expand or 0 to keep it.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SelectLine
functions.description: Selects a line in current script. Line number can be be negative to select a line relative to end of script.

parameters.name: Line
parameters.description: The new line number. If negative, will be relative to end.

parameters.name: Scroll
parameters.description: Whether to scroll to move row visible.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SelectLines
functions.description: Selects lines in current script. Line number can be be negative to select a line relative to end of script.

parameters.name: Line1
parameters.description: The first line number. If negative, will be relative to end.

parameters.name: Line2
parameters.description: The last line number. If negative, will be relative to end.

examples.label: Select rows 5 to 10:
examples.xml_code:

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.SelectLines"; 5; 10 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Select last two lines:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.SelectLines"; -2; -1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SelectedLine
functions.description: Queries selected line.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SelectedLines
functions.description: Queries selected line numbers. If ranges is 1, we return ranges like "3-5¶7¶10-12", otherwise we return "3¶4¶5¶7¶10¶11¶12".

parameters.name: Ranges
parameters.description: Whether to return list with ranges. Pass 1 for ranges or 0 for just line numbers.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SetBackgroundColor
functions.description: Sets background color of script workspace. A fun function to set color if needed, e.g. to highlight the script or make two ScriptWorkspaces have different colors.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: The color specification. This can be a common color name or color in hex notation: # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Set to yellow:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.SetBackgroundColor"; "#FFFFDD" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SetFocusToScriptList
functions.description: Set the focus to the script list in the Script Workspace. This may help to make sure copy & paste commands to the script list target the scripts.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.SetScriptListSearch
functions.description: Sets search field in script list in Script Workspace. Performs also search after changing text.

parameters.name: SearchText
parameters.description: The text to put in the search field.

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.StepsVisible
functions.description: Queries whether steps are visible.

examples.label: Queries state:
examples.text_code:
Show Custom Dialog [ "Scripts: " &
MBS
( "
ScriptWorkspace.ScriptsVisible
" ) & ", Steps: "&
MBS
( "ScriptWorkspace.StepsVisible" )]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Scripts: " & MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.ScriptsVisible" ) & ", Steps: "& MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.StepsVisible" )</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.StyledScriptText
functions.description: Queries text of current script. Can return text as styled text, plain text, html or rtf. Maybe useful for automation workflows where you like to evaluate an expression by hotkey which puts current scripttext into a CMS for your website or writes to it to a text file to compare differences later.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The mode to use. Can be HTML, RTF, StyledText or Text. Default is StyledText.

parameters.name: SelectedOnly
parameters.description: Return only selected text lines. Default is 0.

parameters.name: EndOfLine
parameters.description: The character for end of line within script steps, e.g. multi line comments. If not specified or empty, defaults to Char(13).

examples.label: Query text as styled text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ScriptWorkspace.StyledScriptText"; "StyledText")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScriptWorkspace.Window
functions.description: Queries window ID for the Script Workspace. Allows to use other commands like Window.Hide .
functions.result: Returns window reference number or error.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AddContextMenuCommand
functions.signature: MBS( "SyntaxColoring.AddContextMenuCommand"; Title; Expression { ; InstallWhen; keyEquivalent; KeyEquivalentModifierMask; AvailabilityExpression } )
functions.description: Adds a new command for contextual menu. Given commands are added to contextual menu in Script Workspace when right clicking on script steps. Commands are stored in preferences file and can be removed via SyntaxColoring.ClearContextMenuCommands function. If you use Clipboard.GetFileMakerData and Clipboard.SetFileMakerData functions, you can get the XML for the current step. You may want to use Menubar.RunMenuCommand to run cut/copy/paste/delete commands from the FileMaker menu with the respective command IDs: 57635 for cut, 57634 for copy, 57637 for paste, 57632 for delete and 57642 for select all. Possible uses include commands to add new script steps. to check scripts for errors. to search and replace things to add common snippets, possibly using SQL or text file functions to read the XML for them. to pass script steps as XML to external tools. Use SyntaxColoring.RemoveContextMenuCommand to remove one or SyntaxColoring.ClearContextMenuCommands to clear all.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title for the menu command. If multi line, we build submenus based on the path descripted. If title is just "-", we add a separator.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate in FileMaker when menu command is choosen.

parameters.name: InstallWhen
parameters.description: Whether to enable only if selection is present. 0: always on (default) 1: need no row selected 2: need exactly one row selected 3: need one or more rows selected 4: need all rows selected Add 8 to allow for discontinuous selections (in version 10.0 or later). 16: Show in browse mode. 32: Show in layout mode. 64: Show in Script Workspace For compatibilty we also show items in the script workspace, where none of the three flags above is set.

parameters.name: keyEquivalent
parameters.description: The key equivalent. Is shown right in the menu and can be pressed while menu is open.

parameters.name: KeyEquivalentModifierMask
parameters.description: The menu items's keyboard equivalent modifiers. NSShiftKeyMask is a valid modifier for any key equivalent in mask. This allows you to specify key-equivalents such as Command-Shift-1 that are consistent across all keyboards. However, with a few exceptions (such as the German "ß” character), a lowercase character with NSShiftKeyMask is interpreted the same as the uppercase character without that mask. For example, Command-Shift-c and Command-C are considered to be identical key equivalents. NSShiftKeyMask	131072	Shift Key NSControlKeyMask	262144	Control Key NSAlternateKeyMask	524288	Alternate/Option Key NSCommandKeyMask	1048576	Command Key

parameters.name: AvailabilityExpression
parameters.description: An optional expression to calculate when building the menu. The expression needs to return a boolean. Return 0 to hide menu entry or 1 to show it. If the expression won't compile, we hide the menu entry. Trace function will log the expressions with results to help debugging. To speed up menu building, we cache results, so if multiple entries hve the same calculation, we only perform it once.

examples.label: Add a greeting command:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.AddContextMenuCommand"; "Greeting"; "MBS(\"Msgbox\"; \"Hello World\")"; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add a search and replace command:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "SyntaxColoring.AddContextMenuCommand";
"Search and Replace";
"Let ([
/* ask for search text via dialog */
ResetResult =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.Reset
\" );
ClearResult =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.ClearFields
\" );
MessageResult =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.SetMessage
\"; \"Search and replace in script:\" );
InfoResult =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.SetInformativeText
\"; \"Search text can include XML tags.\" );
Field1Result =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.AddField
\"; \"Search for:\" );
Field2Result =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.AddField
\"; \"Replace with:\" );
Button1Result =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.SetDefaultButton
\"; \"Replace\");
Button2Result =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.SetAlternateButton
\"; \"Cancel\");
DialogResult =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.Run
\" );
SearchText =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.GetFieldText
\"; 0 );
ReplaceText =
MBS
( \"
Dialog.GetFieldText
\"; 1 );
/* do replace */
r = If(DialogResult = \"Replace\"; Let([
/* copy script steps */
r =
MBS
( \"
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
\"; 57634 );
/* get XML from clipboard */
xml =
MBS
( \"
Clipboard.GetFileMakerData
\"; \"ScriptStep\" );
/* search and replace */
xml = Substitute ( xml; SearchText; ReplaceText);
/* put XML back on clipboard */
r =
MBS
( \"
Clipboard.SetFileMakerData
\"; \"ScriptStep\"; xml );
/* paste script steps */
r =
MBS
( \"
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
\"; 57637 )
]; 1))
];1)"; 3 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.AddContextMenuCommand"; 
"Search and Replace"; 
"Let ([ 
/* ask for search text via dialog */
ResetResult = MBS( \"Dialog.Reset\" );
ClearResult = MBS( \"Dialog.ClearFields\" );
MessageResult = MBS( \"Dialog.SetMessage\"; \"Search and replace in script:\" );
InfoResult = MBS( \"Dialog.SetInformativeText\"; \"Search text can include XML tags.\" );
Field1Result = MBS( \"Dialog.AddField\"; \"Search for:\" );
Field2Result = MBS( \"Dialog.AddField\"; \"Replace with:\" );
Button1Result = MBS( \"Dialog.SetDefaultButton\"; \"Replace\");
Button2Result = MBS( \"Dialog.SetAlternateButton\"; \"Cancel\");
DialogResult = MBS( \"Dialog.Run\" );
SearchText = MBS( \"Dialog.GetFieldText\"; 0 );
ReplaceText = MBS( \"Dialog.GetFieldText\"; 1 );
/* do replace */
r = If(DialogResult = \"Replace\"; Let([
	/* copy script steps */
	r = MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 57634 ); 
	/* get XML from clipboard */
	xml = MBS( \"Clipboard.GetFileMakerData\"; \"ScriptStep\" ); 
	/* search and replace */
	xml = Substitute ( xml; SearchText; ReplaceText); 
	/* put XML back on clipboard */
	r = MBS( \"Clipboard.SetFileMakerData\"; \"ScriptStep\"; xml ); 
	/* paste script steps */
	r = MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 57637 ) 
]; 1))
];1)"; 3 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add an insert JSONSetElement command:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.AddContextMenuCommand"; 
"Insert set variable for JSONSetElement"; 
"Let ([ 
xml = \"<fmxmlsnippet type=\\\"FMObjectList\\\"><Step enable=\\\"True\\\" id=\\\"141\\\" name=\\\"Variable setzen\\\"><Value><Calculation><![CDATA[JSONSetElement ( \" & Char(10) & \"	\\\"{}\\\" ;\" & Char(10) & \"	[ \\\"keyOrIndexOrPath\\\" ; \\\"value\\\" ; JSONString ] ;\" & Char(10) & \"	[ \\\"keyOrIndexOrPath\\\" ; \\\"value\\\" ; JSONString ]¶)]]></Calculation></Value><Repetition><Calculation><![CDATA[1]]></Calculation></Repetition><Name>$json</Name></Step></fmxmlsnippet>\"; 
r = MBS( \"Clipboard.SetFileMakerData\"; \"ScriptStep\"; xml ); 
r = MBS( \"Menubar.RunMenuCommand\"; 57637 /* paste */ ) 
]; \"\" )"; 0 )

// we use Char(10) to get the new line in the XML. And we use \\\ to escape quotes in escaped calculation.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Path =
MBS

Path.AddPathComponent

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AddTag
functions.description: Adds a new color tag for script step, variable, function or formula coloring. Settings are saved in preferences files and you can use different settings in different FileMaker versions. Works with FileMaker 11 or newer. Default tags defined for script steps:

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: What item to color with this tag. Can be ScriptStep, Function, Variable or Formula. Added wildcards for Formula type in v11.5 to color specific keywords.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The name of the item. Up to five words from the left name of the command.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: The color value. Can be one of a few english color names or "#123456" notation like in html.

    </Value>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteCustomFunctions.GetEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether auto complete is activated for custom functions window.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteCustomFunctions.SetEnabled
functions.description: Sets whether auto complete is activated for custom functions window. Default is enabled.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

; Value:

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteFunctions.GetEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether parameters for auto completed functions is enabled.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteFunctions.SetEnabled
functions.description: Sets whether to auto complete MBS functions names. This only shows if the plugin notices you type with a MBS(" on the left. There may be extra spaces before or after the bracket.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteParameters.GetEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether parameters for auto completed functions is enabled.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteParameters.SetEnabled
functions.description: Sets whether to auto complete parameters for MBS functions names. We only add parameters, if you are at the end of the text for the calculation.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.GetEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether auto complete is enabled.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.GetGlobals
functions.description: Queries the list of known global variables.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.GetLocals
functions.description: Queries the initial list of local variables.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetEnabled
functions.description: Sets whether to do auto completion for calculation dialogs. MBS Plugin searches the script upwards for up to 500 lines (see SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.SetLimits ) to find variable declarations. This includes Set Variable script step, various Insert script steps and special comments with @variable, @parameter or @constant prefixes. We then assemble a list of possible variable names to present when you type a $ symbol in your calculation dialog. Typing an underscore character seems to complete the word right away and may lead to an extra underscore character, which you need to remove manually. For custom functions or specify output file dialog, you need to manually trigger auto complete. This seems to happen with Option-ESC shortcut on macOS Sonoma and with F5 in older versions (if dictation is off).

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Enable it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetEnabled"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Disable Auto Complete:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetEnabled"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetGlobals
functions.description: Sets initial list of known global variables. When the plugin sees more global variables, it will increase the list. This function allows you to pre-populate the list for global variables for auto complete on start of your solution.

parameters.name: Globals
parameters.description: The list of variable names without $$ prefixes.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetGlobals"; "Hello¶World" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetLocals
functions.description: Sets the initial list of local variables. Put here variables names, which should always show up in auto complete. This function allows you to pre-populate the list for local variables for auto complete on start of your solution. See also SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetGlobals .

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of variable names.

examples.label: Add error to the list of local variables:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SyntaxColoring.AutoCompleteVariables.SetLocals"; "error")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Disable
functions.description: Disables checking variable declarations in FileMaker Scripts. Settings are saved in preferences files and you can use different settings in different FileMaker versions. Works with FileMaker 11 or newer. Checking variable declarations can only be enabled in FileMaker Pro, not in server or runtime versions. This setting can be set via the preferences dialog, too.

examples.label: Enable or disable checking variable declarations:
examples.text_code:
If (Enable)
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Enable
" )]
else
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Disable" )]
end if
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If (Enable)
   Set Variable [$r; MBS( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Enable" )]
else
   Set Variable [$r; MBS( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Disable" )]
end if</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Declare variable via comment:
examples.text_code:
# Declare variables via comment as plugin has no way to know easily that
$param1
is defined here
# @parameter
$param1
# @parameter
$param2
Set Variable [
$r
; Value: Let ( [ parameters = Get(ScriptParameter);
$param1
= GetValue(parameters; 1);
$param2
= GetValue(parameters; 2) ] ; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Declare variables via comment as plugin has no way to know easily that $param1 is defined here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> @parameter $param1</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> @parameter $param2</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ parameters = Get(ScriptParameter); $param1 = GetValue(parameters; 1); $param2 = GetValue(parameters; 2) ] ; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Enable
functions.description: Enables checking variable declarations in FileMaker Scripts. Settings are saved in preferences files and you can use different settings in different FileMaker versions. Works with FileMaker 11 or newer. Checking variable declarations can only be enabled in FileMaker Pro, not in server or runtime versions. By default this is off. It takes some additional CPU time for drawing the script and not everyone may want to have it on. Of course the extra work for the CPU  should not be noticeable to the developer. Variable names declared inside Let commands are not recognized. May only work in Advanced version of FileMaker. You can declare variables in comment when writing @parameter, @variable or @constant in the comment followed by variable name. Shorter @param, @var and @const also work and you can declare multiple variables in one line. On macOS Ventura, when you scroll with errors shown, you may see redraw issues.

examples.label: Enable or disable checking variable declarations:
examples.text_code:
If (Enable)
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Enable" )]
else
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "
SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Disable
" )]
end if
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If (Enable)
   Set Variable [$r; MBS( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Enable" )]
else
   Set Variable [$r; MBS( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Disable" )]
end if</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.GetLimits
functions.description: Queries limits for variable check. By default this returns 500 and 2000 as we look up to 500 lines up for variable declaration and don't check scripts with 2000 or more lines.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Notification.Disable
functions.description: Disables notifications sent by variable check.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Notification.Enable
functions.description: Enables notifications sent by variable check. If enabled, the plugin will notify you automatically about missing variables.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.Notification.Enabled
functions.description: Queries whether notifications are enabled. If enabled, the plugin will notify you automatically about missing variables.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.SetLimits
functions.description: Sets limits for variable check. By default this returns 500 and 2000 as we look up to 500 lines up for variable declaration and don't check scripts with 2000 or more lines. Script workspace gets slower if we check >500 lines on every change made. It may be good to repeat the variable declarations with @variable comments instead of increasing the limits.

parameters.name: Limit1
parameters.description: How many lines to look up.

parameters.name: Limit2
parameters.description: How long a script may be to not be checked at all.

examples.label: Set higher limits:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.SetLimits"; 600; 2400 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Reduce to half of the deault:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SyntaxColoring.CheckVariableDeclaration.SetLimits"; 250; 1000 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.ClipboardConverter.GetEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether the clipboard converter is enabled. Returns 1 if enabled or 0 if disabled.

functions.name: SyntaxColoring.ClipboardConverter.SetEnabled
functions.description: Sets whether the clipboard converter is enabled. If enabled, we convert between XML text and FileMaker's XML. You copy something in FileMaker like a couple of script steps, then switch to a text editor and do some search and replace. Then copy the text again and move to FileMaker to paste it. If the clipboard doesn't contain XML in the right style, we do nothing. This applies to copying layouts, script steps, scripts, custom functions, fields, tables and value lists.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Pass 1 to turn on and 0 to turn off.

examples.label: Enable this feature:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("SyntaxColoring.ClipboardConverter.SetEnabled"; 1) ]
Example result:
OK
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SyntaxColoring.ClipboardConverter.SetEnabled"; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Diesable this feature:
examples.text_code:

$r
; Value:

("SyntaxColoring.ClipboardConverter.SetEnabled"; 0) ]
Example result:
OK
examples.xml_code:
